summary.intent: Implement a native ReadSkillTool for the moltis gateway that lets agents read skill files without leaking absolute paths, add a prompt-injection safety scanner, and update the system-prompt to reference the new tool. Then enhance ReadSkillTool with hermes-agent-inspired features and fix PR review comments.
summary.outcome: PR #630 was opened, pushed, and iteratively improved across three commits (66ad3c22, 212c39eb, plus a PR-fixes commit). All five planned beads issues were closed; a follow-up issue for hermes-ported features was also opened, completed, and closed. 58 skill_tools unit tests, 11 safety tests, 8 gateway integration tests, and 109 skills tests all pass; targeted clippy clean on all four affected crates.

repo: Skills discovery uses FsSkillDiscoverer shared across tool instances in gateway/src/server.rs; new skill tools must be registered in the skill-tools block there.
Path containment for skill files is enforced via normalize_relative_skill_file_path (reusable guard already present in skill_tools.rs); new tools should reuse it rather than rolling their own.
The prompt_gen.rs <available_skills> block previously leaked absolute SKILL.md paths via a path= attribute — this was the root bug motivating the plan.
The hermes-agent safety scanner (9 injection patterns) was already available for porting; it lives in crates/skills/src/safety.rs after this session.
moltis-csq is a pre-existing macOS llama-cpp-sys CUDA build failure that blocks `just lint` but is unrelated to any changes in this workspace; targeted per-crate clippy is the reliable alternative.
Sidecar directories follow the agentskills.io convention: references/, templates/, scripts/, assets/ — assets/ was missing from the original implementation and added via the hermes follow-up.
Frontmatter stripping for plugin-injected content needed a tolerant strip_optional_frontmatter helper added to crates/skills/src/parse.rs to avoid leaking raw YAML into agent responses.
Dolt remote is not configured in the robust-monday worktree, so `bd dolt pull` fails; commits are local-only until pushed to GitHub.

code.finding: ReadSkillTool primary read path must call strip_optional_frontmatter (from parse.rs) before returning body content to avoid leaking raw plugin frontmatter YAML to the agent.
read_sidecar must reject symlinked skill directories (fs::symlink_metadata check before descending) to prevent escape via symlink chains, not just symlinked individual files.
The activation instruction for read_skill must mention assets/ alongside references/, templates/, scripts/ to match the actual sidecar surface area; tests assert this.
The path-leak assertion used a broad || condition across test cases; PR review requested tightening it to a per-case assert so failures are unambiguous.
New strip_optional_frontmatter helper added: returns body after YAML front-matter block if present, otherwise returns the full string unchanged — tolerant of missing or malformed front-matter.
Integration test seeding pattern: create a tempdir, write a minimal skill structure, point FsSkillDiscoverer at it, instantiate ReadSkillTool, and call execute() directly — no HTTP layer needed.

workflow: Beads issues were claimed before starting each sub-task and closed immediately after tests passed, keeping epic progress accurate throughout the session.
Background Bash tasks (clippy, integration tests) were launched in parallel with editing work; task-notification messages were used to confirm results without blocking.
Targeted per-crate clippy (cargo clippy -p moltis-skills -p moltis-tools etc.) is the reliable lint path when just lint is blocked by an unrelated build failure.
PR description was updated after the follow-up commit to cover both commits, keeping reviewers informed without opening a second PR.
cargo fmt --check was run before every commit; empty output = clean.

friction: just lint is permanently broken on macOS due to moltis-csq (llama-cpp-sys CUDA build failure), forcing targeted per-crate clippy as a workaround every session.
Dolt remote is not configured in the robust-monday worktree, so `bd dolt pull` fails silently — no sync of beads state back to the remote Dolt instance.
Background task-notification summaries can be misleading: bcalmwoi9 was labeled 'exit code 0' by the notification but actually exited 101; had to manually check the output file.
The symlink-escape rejection for sidecar reads was initially only applied to individual files, not the skill directory itself — caught by PR review, not by the original tests.
open_items: moltis-avv (token-budget fallback for skill reads) is intentionally left open; the plan explicitly deferred it.
skills_list and skills_categories progressive-disclosure tools (ported from hermes-agent) are out of scope for this PR — maps to moltis-avv or a new issue.
Platform gating in skill frontmatter (hermes feature: only surface skills compatible with current platform) requires a frontmatter spec change not yet defined.
Required env-var validation for skills (hermes feature: warn if a skill's declared env deps are missing) was explicitly deferred.
Slashy path resolution (hermes: resolve /skill-name shorthand to the canonical skill directory) was explicitly deferred.
moltis-csq (macOS llama-cpp-sys CUDA build failure) blocks full just lint runs and should be fixed independently.
